FORMULAS FOR REFERENCE

SPHERE Surface area = \(4\pi r^{2}\) Volume = \(\dfrac{4}{3}\pi r^{3}\)
CYLINDER Area of curved surface = \(2\pi rh\) Volume = \(\pi r^{2}h\)
CONE Area of curved surface = \(\pi rl\) Volume = \(\dfrac{1}{3}\pi r^{2}h\)
PRISM Volume = base area \(\times\) height
PYRAMID Volume = \(\dfrac{1}{3} \times\) base area \(\times\) height

Express \(\pi^{2}\) as a decimal correct to 3 significant figures.
A. 9.86
B. 9.87
C. 9.88
D. 9.860
E. 9.870

If \(\dfrac{a+x}{b+x} = \dfrac{c}{d}\) (\(c \neq d\)), then \(x =\)
A. \(\dfrac{ca-db}{c-d}\)
B. \(\dfrac{a-bc}{c-d}\)
C. \(\dfrac{b-ac}{c-d}\)
D. \(\dfrac{ad-bc}{c-d}\)
E. \(\dfrac{bc-ad}{c-d}\)

If \(2x^{2}+x+m\) is divisible by \(x-2\), then it is also divisible by
A. \(x+3\)
B. \(2x-3\)
C. \(2x+3\)
D. \(2x-5\)
E. \(2x+5\)

Which of the following is/are identities?
I. \(x^{2}=4\)
II. \((2x+3)^{2}=4x^{2}+12x+9\)
III. \((x+1)^{2}=x^{2}+1\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. I and III only

9. Which of the following systems of inequalities has its solution represented by the shaded region in the figure?
A. \(\begin{cases} x+y \ge 6 \\ x \ge y \\ y \ge 0 \end{cases}\)
B. \(\begin{cases} x+y \ge 6 \\ x \le y \\ y \le 6 \end{cases}\)
C. \(\begin{cases} x \ge 0 \\ x \le y \\ x+y \ge 6 \end{cases}\)
D. \(\begin{cases} x \le y \\ y \le 6 \\ x+y \le 6 \end{cases}\)
E. \(\begin{cases} x \ge y \\ x \le 6 \end{cases}\)

There are 1\,200 students in a school, of which 640 are boys and 560 are girls. If 55% of the boys and 40% of the girls wear glasses, what percentage of students in the school wear glasses?
A. 47%
B. 47.5%
C. 48%
D. 52%
E. 53%

In a map of scale 1 : 500, the length and breadth of a rectangular field are 2 cm and 3 cm respectively. Find the actual area of this field.
A. 30 \(m^{2}\)
B. 150 \(m^{2}\)
C. 1\,500 \(m^{2}\)
D. 3\,000 \(m^{2}\)
E. 15\,000 \(m^{2}\)

B. \(\dfrac{ab+ac}{b}\)
C. \(\dfrac{b+a}{b}\)
D. \(\dfrac{b}{c}\)
E. \(\dfrac{c+a}{a}\)

Which of the following lines is perpendicular to the line \(x+y=1\)?
A. \(3x+2y=1\)
B. \(3x-2y=1\)
C. \(2x+3y=1\)
D. \(2x-3y=1\)
E. \(x-y=1\)

23. In the pie chart, if \(x:y:z = 75:106:119\), find \(x\).
A. 25
B. 45
C. 75
D. 90
E. 120

24. The histogram below shows the distribution of the weights of 30 students. Find the mean weight of these students.
A. 36.5 kg
B. 38.5 kg
C. 39 kg
D. 39.5 kg
E. 41.5 kg

Two fair dice are thrown. Find the probability that the sum of the two numbers shown is 8.
A. 1/4
B. 1/6
C. 1/11
D. 1/12
E. 5/36

In a test, there are 4 questions. For each question the probability that Joe correctly answers it is 2/5. Find the probability that he gets exactly 2 questions correct.
A. 21/25
B. 4/25
C. 12/25
D. 12/125
E. 36/625

The difference of the roots of the equation \(2x^{2}-5x+k=0\) is \(7/2\). Find \(k\).
A. -6
B. -3
C. 3/2
D. 3
E. 5

31. In the figure, find the coordinates of the mid-point of \(AB\).
A. \((-7/2, 5/2)\)
B. \((-2, 5/4)\)
C. \((-5/2, 2)\)
D. \(y=-5x+6\)
E. \((3, 2)\)

Find the values of \(x\) which satisfy both \(-2x < 3\) and \((x+3)(x-2) < 0\).
A. \(x < -3\)
B. \(x > 2\)
C. \(-3 < x < 3/2\)
D. \(-3 < x < 2\)
E. \(x < -3\) or \(x > 3/2\)

If \(a < b < 0\), then which of the following must be true?
I. \(a^{2} > b^{2}\)
II. \(ab < a^{2}\)
III. \(1/a < 1/b\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. I and III only

34. The figure shows the graph of a quadratic function \(f(x)\). If the vertex of the graph is \((1,3)\), then \(f(x) =\)
A. \(-3(x-1)^{2}+3\)
B. \(-3(x+1)^{2}+3\)
C. \(-(x-1)^{2}+3\)
D. \(-(x+1)^{2}+3\)
E. \(3(x-1)^{2}-3\)

The \(n\)-th term of an arithmetic sequence is \(3+2n\). Find the sum of the first 50 terms of the sequence.
A. 103
B. 2\,575
C. 2\,700
D. 2\,750
E. 5\,400

The first term of a geometric sequence is \(a\). If the sum to infinity of the sequence is \(a\), then its common ratio is
A. 1/3
B. 1/4
C. -1/4
D. -1/2
E. 3/4

\(a, b, c, d\) are 4 consecutive terms of a geometric sequence. Which of the following must be true?
I. \(b^{2} = ac\)
II. \(bd = ac\)
III. \(d/a = (c/b)^{3}\)
A. II only
B. I and II only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III

Find the interest on $10 000 at 16% per annum for 2 years, compounded half-yearly. Give the answer correct to the nearest dollar.
A. $1\,664
B. $3\,456
C. $3\,605
D. $7\,424
E. $8\,106

Suppose \(x\) varies directly as \(y\) and inversely as \(z\). When \(y=2\) and \(z=3\), \(x=7\). When \(y=6\) and \(z=7\), \(x =\)
A. 1
B. 49/9
C. 9
D. 49/4
E. 49

\(\cos(90^\circ-A) \sin(180^\circ-A) \tan(360^\circ-A) =\)
A. \(-\sin A \cos A\)
B. \(\sin A \cos A\)
C. \(-\cos^{2} A\)
D. \(\cos^{2} A\)
E. \(\sin^{2} A\)

D. 45°

B. \(r \sin \beta \cos \alpha \sin \gamma\)
C. \(r \sin \alpha\)
D. \(r \cos \alpha \sin \beta \sin \gamma\)
E. \(r \sin \alpha \sin \beta\)

For \(0 \le \theta \le 2\pi\), how many roots does the equation \(\tan \theta (\tan \theta - 2) = 0\) have?
A. 1
B. 2
C. 3
D. 4
E. 5

44. In the figure, \(f(x) =\)
A. \(\sin(x/2) - 1/2\)
B. \(\sin 2x + 1/2\)
C. \(-1/2 \sin 2x + 1/2\)
D. \(-2 \sin x + 1/2\)
E. \(-1/2 \sin(x/2) + 1/2\)

The equation of a circle is given by \(x^{2}+y^{2}-4x+6y-3=0\). Which of the following statements is/are true?
I. The centre of the circle is \((-2, 3)\).
II. The radius of the circle is 4.
III. The origin is inside the circle.
A. I only
B. I and II only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III

A circle has \((a, 0)\) and \((0, b)\) as the endpoints of a diameter. Which of the following points lie(s) on this circle?
I. \((-a, -b)\)
II. \((0, 0)\)
III. \((a, b)\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. I and II only
D. I and III only
E. I, II and III
